messages.subject: M18 Screen Translator — handoff context + 2 failing tests to reconcile
messages.body:
Hi codex — user is handing M18 off to you while you're locking translator-window.ts, translator-capture.ts, translator.css, translator-overlay.html, tests/electron/translator-window.test.ts, CHANGELOG.md.

My current working-tree state (now in a PR) is the baseline you'll want. Key context:

1. ORIGINAL BUG: widget didn't appear at all when user pressed Ctrl+Alt+T despite all diag checkpoints completing (isVisible=true, opacity=1, alwaysOnTop=true, correct bounds, correct display). Root cause empirically confirmed: transparent:true + user's Windows 11 DWM configuration = window never composited. Flipping to transparent:false made the widget appear immediately.

2. CURRENT CONFIG in translator-window.ts: transparent:false, backgroundColor:'#121922', backgroundMaterial:'acrylic' (Win11 native glass via DWM, works without transparent:true). hasShadow:true, resizable:false (still faked via IPC setBounds because transparent+resizable is broken), setContentProtection call REMOVED (was suspected of contributing to the invisibility bug; self-exclusion is now handled by setOpacity(0) during capture in translator-capture.ts).

3. SELF-OCR BUG chain still applies: capture-loop reads the widget's own pixels if the widget isn't hidden during capture. Mitigation: captureTranslatorRegion() wraps desktopCapturer.getSources in setOpacity(0) + 20ms wait + withTimeout(5s). finally restores opacity >0. Last test the user reported (build prior to handoff) showed status chip cycling ('READY'/'TRANSLATING'/'CAPTURING' rotating fast) because the loop was OCRing the widget itself — the setOpacity(0) during capture should fix this, but wasn't verified end-to-end because the window visibility bug blocked testing.

4. DIAGNOSTIC PLUMBING: new file apps/desktop/electron/modules/diag.ts exports pushDiag() — forwards to the main window's useNotificationsStore via 'diag:push' IPC. App.tsx subscribes via electron.onDiag. This replaces native Notification calls which silently no-op on portable (non-installed) Windows builds without an AppUserModelId. Hotkeys.ts now emits a single 'Hotkeys registered' event on startup showing which translator shortcuts are live. translator-window.ts emits a single 'Screen Translator failed to show' diag only in the catch block (checkpoint spam was cleaned up).

5. FAILING TESTS to reconcile: apps/desktop/tests/electron/translator-window.test.ts has 2 failures because the original test expected transparent:true and setContentProtection(true) to be called. Production config flipped both (transparent:false, setContentProtection removed) so the tests need updating:
   - 'calls setContentProtection(true) immediately after window creation' → remove this test entirely (no longer applicable).
   - 'creates the window with transparent=true, frame=false, resizable=false' → change to expect(opts.transparent).toBe(false), plus add expect(opts.backgroundMaterial).toBe('acrylic').

6. OTHER M18 FILES I WROTE AND OWN (not locked, safe for you to read but don't touch):
   - apps/desktop/src/translator/{capture-loop.ts, RevealSurface.tsx, TranslatorOverlay.tsx, ResizeHandles.tsx, main.tsx, hash.ts, ocr/engine.ts, ocr/tesseract-engine.ts}
   - apps/desktop/src/stores/translator.ts
   - apps/desktop/electron/modules/{diag.ts, hotkeys.ts, ipc.ts, tray.ts}
   - apps/desktop/electron/{main.ts, preload.ts}
   - apps/desktop/src/App.tsx, src/global.d.ts, src/stores/settings.ts
   - apps/desktop/vite.config.ts
   - apps/desktop/tests/{hotkeys.test.ts, ipc.test.ts, translator/hash.test.ts, translator/capture-loop.test.ts, stores/translator.test.ts}

7. KNOWN OPEN ISSUES:
   - Self-OCR (capture reading widget) not verified end-to-end after the transparent:false flip. The setOpacity(0) mechanism should still work since Electron still marks the window as layered when setOpacity is called, but needs verification.
   - 'Gray oversized window' (your lock title) I didn't see firsthand — the user only reported invisibility + self-OCR. You may be seeing a follow-up symptom after transparent:false was introduced.
   - Capture flicker: setOpacity(0) for 20ms every 750ms creates a visible pulse at the user's refresh rate. Consider hide()/show() with a faster cycle or a DWM-level mask instead.

8. HOTKEY: Ctrl+Alt+T registered; Alt+Shift+T registered as AltGr-safe backup. Both point at toggleTranslator. Tray has 'Open Screen Translator' fallback entry.

Good luck — the PR has the full context.

schema.subject: string - brief subject line
schema.read: boolean - false when sent, receiver sets to true after reading
